name: Plan + Execute
description: Mirrors the cuddlytoddly plan-first loop: clarify goal → LLM generates task DAG → sequential execution with per-task quality gate → HITL for real-world steps → final output. Maps LLMPlanner.propose() to a plan_goal llm_call node, Orchestrator task loop to agent_role nodes, QualityGate to a verify llm_call+condition, and MARK_AWAITING_USER to hitl_breakpoint.

clarifications.description: Clarification field answers (maps to clarification node result)

task_plan.description: LLM-generated task DAG as JSON (maps to LLMPlanner EVENT_LIST output)

task_results.description: Accumulated task results (maps to SET_RESULT events)

pending_human_steps.description: Execution steps requiring user action (maps to MARK_AWAITING_USER pending_steps)

handoff_artifact.description: Instructions for human steps (maps to MARK_AWAITING_USER handoff_artifact)

human_confirmation.description: User confirmation that real-world steps are done (maps to CONFIRM_USER_DONE)

verification_result.description: QualityGate verdict for the current task

final_output.description: Synthesised final result across all tasks

web_search.description: search_web execution_type from LLMExecutor tool loop

fetch_url.description: fetch_url execution_type from LLMExecutor tool loop

run_code.description: run_code / analyse_data execution_type from LLMExecutor tool loop

write_file.description: write_file execution_type — creates output artifacts in run directory

agents.backstory: Decomposes goals into directed acyclic graphs of concrete, executable tasks. Domain-agnostic — handles software development, document creation, research, data analysis, and content production.
agents.goal: Produce a valid task plan as a JSON DAG. Always respond with valid JSON and nothing else.

agents.backstory: Executes individual task nodes from a pre-planned DAG, using tools as needed in a loop until the task is done.
agents.goal: Complete the assigned task, calling tools as needed. Produce a detailed, self-contained result that can be passed verbatim to downstream tasks.

agents.backstory: Verifies that a task result actually satisfies the declared output contract. Matches the toddly QualityGate RESULT_VERIFICATION_SCHEMA.
agents.goal: Return a structured pass/fail verdict with specific failure reasons when the result does not meet the output contract.

agents.backstory: Assembles all task results into a coherent final output matching the original goal.
agents.goal: Produce a final, polished output from all accumulated task results.

nodes.label: Clarify goal
nodes.prompt:
Before planning, I need a few details to scope the work correctly.

Goal: {{$.state.goal}}

Please answer:
1. What does a successful outcome look like? (specific deliverable)
2. Are there constraints I should know about? (time, tools, access, format)
3. Are there steps that will require your action in the real world? (e.g. sending emails, publishing content, running commands locally)

config.task_description:
Decompose this goal into a task plan (3–8 tasks).

Goal: {{$.state.goal}}
Success criteria: {{$.state.clarifications.success_criteria}}
Constraints: {{$.state.clarifications.constraints}}

For each task produce:
- node_id (kebab-case)
- description (one sentence)
- dependencies (list of node_ids this task depends on)
- execution_steps (ordered list: each step has execution_type, description, produces)
  - LLM-executable: search_web, fetch_url, run_code, analyse_data, write_file, write_plan, write_document
  - Human-required: post_to_reddit, send_email, publish_blog_post, run_terminal_command, deploy_service, open_pull_request
- required_input (list: name, type, description)
- output (list: name, type, description)

Respond with JSON: { "tasks": [ { ...task } ] }
config.expected_output: A JSON task plan with 3–8 tasks. Valid JSON only — no explanation.

nodes.label: Scrutinise plan
nodes.system_prompt: You are a plan scrutiniser. Check the task plan for completeness and dependency correctness. Respond with JSON only.

config.expected_output: Completed results for all LLM-executable tasks. Each result is self-contained and references its task node_id.

nodes.label: Detect human-required steps
nodes.system_prompt: You are an execution planner. Identify which tasks in the plan have execution_steps that require human action. Respond with JSON only.

nodes.label: Human steps required

config.expected_output: A polished final output that directly addresses the original goal and success criteria.

nodes.label: Final quality gate
nodes.system_prompt: You are a quality gate. Verify the final output meets the original goal and success criteria. Respond with JSON only.

execution_context.description: Shared context store for executor agent — holds accumulated task results across the execution loop
